content: Imagine your savings as a tree that not only grows but also produces seeds, which grow into new trees over time. This is how compound interest works—it’s like earning interest on your original money and also on the interest that money has already earned. Instead of just making a small return each year, your savings start to grow faster and faster as time goes on. For example, if you save some money today and leave it untouched, the interest you earn gets added back to your savings. Next time, you earn interest on a bigger amount. Over many years, this “interest on interest” effect can significantly increase your savings without any extra effort on your part. The key is to start early and be patient, allowing time to work its magic. Compound interest turns small, steady savings into substantial wealth, making it one of the most powerful tools for financial growth.
